description: Ability to drive system evolution through architecture and domain expertise.

signals: I successfully push new features and bug fixes to improve and extend the system
I apply domain knowledge while meeting non-functional requirements like usability and security
examples: I enhanced the transaction log UI to display additional data crucial for reconciliation
I fixed a critical bug and verified it meets performance and security constraints

signals: I design and implement medium-to-large features while reducing technical debt
I ensure compliance and alignment with non-functional requirements (NFRs)
examples: I created a new report type for data settlements, collaborating with stakeholders and verifying latency, scalability, and security constraints
I refined requirements and designed the system to meet both business and technical needs

signals: I take responsibility for the system's production operation, monitoring, and SLAs
I proactively address issues and mitigate security or performance risks
examples: When a core service breaks, I detect the issue, assess the impact on SLAs (e.g., transaction throughput, fraud checks), and fix it before customers are affected
I implemented alerting and runbooks for critical services and handled incidents end to end

signals: I evolve the system architecture to support future requirements
I coordinate with other teams and establish well-defined NFRs (performance, security, reliability)
examples: A legacy service couldn't scale horizontally for peak loads. I redesigned it to handle parallel requests and coordinated with other teams to ensure consistent data flows and updated SLAs
I led cross-team alignment on architectural trade-offs to unblock product launches

signals: I drive the technical excellence of the system, making architectural decisions across multiple teams
I facilitate trade-off discussions (e.g., cost vs. performance) and ensure alignment
examples: I designed a new multi-tenant platform architecture, working with domain experts to incorporate regulatory constraints and orchestrating cross-team decisions
I drove architectural decisions that balanced cost, performance, and reliability across the platform
